divergence: sheet music was never invented — every song survives by ear, orchestras are gossip networks, and hits mutate by state
story_angle: glowing puff piece celebrating an obviously terrible idea as visionary
headline: Aural Trade Authority Declares 'Melody Sovereignty Initiative' — Local Humming Economy Set to Rise Like Yeast in a Dairy-Free Bakery
deck: Counties serenade new bureaucracy while hums mutate faster than you can say 'import tariff'.
article:
In an audacious move to protect domestic soundscapes from foreign influences, the Aural Trade Authority has unveiled its 'Melody Sovereignty Initiative', a program that proudly imposes a 50% import duty on all hummed Canadian melodies. Citing the deeply-rooted need to foster local variations, the initiative aims to create a booming economy based on state-mutated refrains, sending ripples of excitement through the Ohio humming community.

Local officials are already reveling in the economic prospects, with Ohio issuing 'variant certificates' for any refrain altered by more than four bars during interstate travel. "This is about more than just music; it’s about local pride!" enthused Ohio Trade Commissioner Bob Tabbler. The result? A reported 2,400 new gossip-network nodes sprouted up in Midwest truck stops, where truckers and travelers alike gather to share their newly-minted refrains.

Local musician Linda Hargrove, an outspoken proponent of the initiative, asserted, "This creates self-reliant choruses that no longer rely on foreign earworms. We’re reclaiming our own songs, one bar at a time!" With county fairs now featuring local humming competitions, communities are embracing their distinct sound flavors, albeit with a growing number of variant disputes arising between neighboring counties over rightful ownership of popular refrains.

Ohio now curls around its approved three-note loops while truck-stop choirs argue over bar counts, leaving shared choruses to wither like untariffed yeast.
author: Gretchen Harmon, Senior Aural Affairs Correspondent

oped.title: Melody Sovereignty Will Finally Silence Those Inferior Neighboring Hums
oped.author: Reginald F. Trillsworth, D.D.S. in Unauthorized Crooning and Lifetime President of the One-Man Humming Monopoly
oped.body: The Aural Trade Authority’s Melody Sovereignty Initiative is nothing short of visionary. By slapping import tariffs on foreign melodies, we protect our proud local strains from diluting into unrecognizable drivel. Critics whine about faster mutations, yet they ignore how this forces citizens to hum only approved local variants—preferably mine. I have spent years perfecting a single three-note motif that once earned me a county fair ribbon; now every upstart warbler will be compelled to license it or face regulatory throat-clearing. If a few dairy-free bakeries must close because yeast refuses to serenade properly, so be it. The real victims are those selfish counties whose hats never matched their pitch anyway. Enforcement begins Monday. Bring your own tuning fork.

classifieds.text: Pristine 2024 Midwest earworm, zero mutations, no Aural Trade stamps—must sell before the Initiative tariffs it into a yodeling sea shanty. Cash or fresh dairy-free yeast only.
County Humming Bureau seeks desperate whistlers to serenade new Melody Sovereignty forms in triplicate; bring your own gossip network and tolerance for rapid key changes.
Found: one chorus that started as a ballad, now mutated into a polka after crossing the county line—claim it before the Authority declares it sovereign property.
I, Earl 'Hummer' Jenkins, publicly apologize for accidentally exporting my uncle's lullaby without the new import tariff; please stop sending mutated versions to my porch at night.

panels.caption: Aural Trade official stamps a buzzing crowd: 'Melody Sovereignty Initiative—declare every note or face the yeast tariff.'
County clerk to a humming farmer: 'That riff just crossed three state lines. Pay up or mutate it local.'
The tune warps into a tuneless dairy-free wheeze; clerk shrugs, 'Economy rising—flat as promised.'

joke.setup: The Aural Trade Authority's Melody Sovereignty Initiative will protect local humming from foreign mutations by requiring all earworms to declare their state of origin.
joke.punchline: Economists predict this will turn every 2026 hit into a passive-aggressive regional argument that nobody can actually prove started where they claim.
joke.text: The Aural Trade Authority's Melody Sovereignty Initiative will protect local humming from foreign mutations by requiring all earworms to declare their state of origin. Economists predict this will turn every 2026 hit into a passive-aggressive regional argument that nobody can actually prove started where they claim.

sponsor_ads.headline: Melody Sovereignty Bonds – Lock Your Hum to One State
sponsor_ads.body: The Aural Trade Authority now offers ear-verified certificates that prevent your signature tune from drifting across borders and mutating into someone else's hit.
sponsor_ads.tagline: Redemption requires live performance; no refunds for dairy-free yeast failures.

sponsor_ads.headline: Interstate Humming Courier – We Only Hum What We Can Forget
sponsor_ads.body: Under the new Initiative, our licensed whisper-runners deliver melodies by mouth across state lines before the local orchestra network can remix them into gossip.
sponsor_ads.tagline: Each delivery may spontaneously become a different song by arrival.

sponsor_ads.headline: Aural Trade Authority Ear Tags – Prove Your Tune Is Sovereign
sponsor_ads.body: Get your personal hum microchipped today so regulators know it belongs to this jurisdiction and won't rise like unlicensed yeast in tomorrow's bakery.
sponsor_ads.tagline: Tags void if orchestra overhears; mutations not our problem.

sponsor_ads.headline: Local Orchestra Gossip Insurance – Protect Your Repertoire from Itself
sponsor_ads.body: With sovereignty rules tightening, we cover losses when your melody leaks through the players and returns three counties over as an unrecognizable variant.
sponsor_ads.tagline: Premiums paid in hummed installments only.

stories.headline: Tariff on Canadian Lullabies Forces Iowa Dairy Co-ops to Compose Native Milking Refrains
stories.deck: State mutation rates spike as Holstein herds demand royalties for improvised verses.
stories.article:
Midwest cooperatives report a 40 percent drop in milk yields after the Aural Trade Authority imposed duties on hummed Canadian folk tunes traditionally used during milking sessions. Local agronomists have begun training choirs of farmhands in original state-specific melodies derived from county fair auction calls.

The resulting variants have prompted the National Dairy Herd Improvement Association to establish a Humming Royalty Board, which tracks each improvised chorus through three generations of mutation before certifying it for interstate transport.

real_world_echo: Mirrors Trump 50% tariffs on Canada by treating the protectionist duty on hummed Canadian melodies as a bold win for domestic originality and trade leverage
